channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Direct synthesis of amorphous metal–organic frameworks from nanoclusters
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 08 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71378-z

The synthesis of amorphous metal–organic frameworks is challenging to control rationally. Here, the authors show that direct synthesis using a nanocluster-based approach enables tuneable structural disorder and defect content.
item.dc:title: Direct synthesis of amorphous metal–organic frameworks from nanoclusters
item.dc:creator: Nanna L. Baun
Sabina S. Mortensen
Heloisa N. Bordallo
Luke Keenan
Kirsten M. Ø. Jensen
Adam F. Sapnik

item.title: A principled basis for nonequilibrium network flows
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 08 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71239-9

Caliber Force Theory extends the thermodynamic structure of equilibrium statistical physics to nonequilibrium systems. The framework derives generalized conjugate relations and transport laws, offering a unified approach to modelling dynamics far from equilibrium.
item.dc:title: A principled basis for nonequilibrium network flows
item.dc:creator: Ying-Jen Yang
Ken A. Dill
